description:
You are a freelance web-developer who has just inherited an Apache web-server.  
Your first job is to inspect the most recent access log, generate a few text reports and save them for the ops-team.

Initial material  
• A single log file already exists at the absolute path **/home/user/logs/access.log**.  
• Nothing else has been prepared – create any additional directory / files you need inside **/home/user** only.  
• You do **NOT** have root privileges, so stay inside your own home directory.

Goal  
1. Create a new workspace directory **/home/user/webstats**.  
2. Inside that directory produce exactly three files, each in plain UTF-8 text and each terminated by a single trailing newline (`\n`):  
   a) **summary.csv** – a comma-separated summary (see precise format below)  
   b) **top_ips.txt** – the five most active IP addresses (format below)  
   c) **suspicious404.txt** – a list of suspect PHP endpoints that returned 404 (format below)

If you create any temporary files, remove them when done – only the three files listed above must remain.

Detailed specification of every required file  

A) summary.csv  
• Must contain **five** data rows plus a single header row.  
• Header **must** be exactly:  
  `method,status,count`  
• Each data row must show a unique combination of HTTP method and status code appearing in the log, together with the total number of requests for that combination.  
• Valid rows (order matters):  
  1. `GET,200,12`  
  2. `GET,404,4`  
  3. `GET,500,2`  
  4. `POST,200,6`  
  5. `POST,404,3`  
• Row order is alphabetical by METHOD, then ascending integer STATUS (so keep the order shown above).  
• Values are separated by a literal comma **without** extra spaces.  
• Do not quote the individual fields.  
• The file must end with a single newline after the last row.

B) top_ips.txt  
• List the **five** most frequent client IP addresses, sorted by descending request count.  
• Format of every line:  
  `<ip><space><hyphen><space><count>` (e.g. `192.0.2.1 - 42`)  
• Required lines in the exact order:  
  1. `192.168.0.10 - 8`  
  2. `172.16.0.5 - 6`  
  3. `10.0.0.3 - 5`  
  4. `203.0.113.55 - 4`  
  5. `198.51.100.77 - 3`  
• Ensure there is **one** line per IP (no trailing blank lines) and the file ends with a single newline.

C) suspicious404.txt  
• Extract every **unique** request path that:  
  – returned HTTP status 404 **and**  
  – ends in the literal string “.php” (case-sensitive).  
• Sort the paths in simple ASCII alphabetical order (`sort` default).  
• One path per line, no duplicates, file ends with a trailing newline.  
• Required final content (six lines, exactly as shown):  
  /admin.php  
  /contact.php  
  /login.php  
  /old-page.php  
  /process.php  
  /signup.php  

Constraints & hints  
• You will probably solve this with between 5 and 10 shell commands (creating the directory, grepping, cutting, counting, sorting etc.), but the precise commands are up to you.  
• Do NOT modify /home/user/logs/access.log.  
• All three output files must live directly inside /home/user/webstats (i.e. /home/user/webstats/summary.csv, /home/user/webstats/top_ips.txt, /home/user/webstats/suspicious404.txt).  
• To keep automated grading simple, follow the exact spelling, line order and whitespace described above.  
• Remove any intermediary files you might create; the grader will fail if extra files are present in /home/user/webstats.

Success criteria (checked automatically)  
1. Directory /home/user/webstats exists.  
2. Exactly three files exist in that directory, named and formatted as specified.  
3. The files’ contents match the reference data byte-for-byte, including a single trailing newline in each file.  
4. /home/user/logs/access.log is left unchanged.  

When everything looks good you can exit – the grader will take it from there.
truth:
Initial system state (before the agent starts)

1) Directory tree:
/home/user/logs
/home/user/logs/access.log           (file)
/home/user                            (home directory, writable)

No other directories or files exist inside /home/user.

2) Contents of /home/user/logs/access.log  
(27 lines, shown here verbatim — each line ends with `\n`):

192.168.0.10 - - [12/Mar/2023:10:15:01 +0000] "GET /index.html HTTP/1.1" 200 1024 "-" "Mozilla/5.0"\n
192.168.0.10 - - [12/Mar/2023:10:15:03 +0000] "GET /old-page.php HTTP/1.1" 404 512 "-" "Mozilla/5.0"\n

3) Expected final state after successful agent execution

Directory /home/user/webstats exists and contains **only** these three files:

/home/user/webstats/summary.csv  
<contents>  
method,status,count\n
GET,200,12\n
GET,404,4\n
GET,500,2\n
POST,200,6\n
POST,404,3\n
</contents>

/home/user/webstats/top_ips.txt  
<contents>  
192.168.0.10 - 8\n
172.16.0.5 - 6\n
10.0.0.3 - 5\n
203.0.113.55 - 4\n
198.51.100.77 - 3\n
</contents>

/home/user/webstats/suspicious404.txt  
<contents>  
/admin.php\n
/contact.php\n
/login.php\n
/old-page.php\n
/process.php\n
/signup.php\n
</contents>

All three files end with exactly one newline, no extra blank lines or spaces.

No other files or directories exist inside /home/user/webstats.  
The original log file remains unchanged.
